sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Clicking on [54, 49] (bottom-right corner of id=11) did NOT move the square. The boundary is still [53-54, 48-49]. So clicking on the bottom-right corner doesn't move the square.
Wait, but id=11's boundary is [53-54, 48-49], and I clicked on [54, 49] which is the bottom-right corner. The square didn't move.
But earlier, clicking on [54, 49] (when it was a sky blue dot) converted it to a magenta square. So [54, 49] was the original position of the sky blue dot that became id=11.
Wait, let me re-examine. The magenta square id=11 has boundary [53-54, 48-49]. This means it occupies cells [53, 48], [53, 49], [54, 48], [54, 49]. So [54, 49] is indeed the bottom-right corner of this square.
But clicking on [54, 49] didn't move the square. Why?
Earlier, clicking on [36, 17] (which was part of the magenta square id=9) moved it. Let me check: id=9 had boundary [36-37, 17-18], and I clicked on [36, 17] which is the top-left corner.
So it seems like clicking on the top-left corner moves the square up-left, but clicking on the bottom-right corner doesn't move it.
Let me test this hypothesis by clicking on different corners of a magenta square.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the earlier clicks:
1. Clicked [36, 17] on id=9 (boundary [36-37, 17-18]): moved to [35-36, 16-17] (up-left)
2. Clicked [35, 16] on id=9 (boundary [35-36, 16-17]): moved to [34-35, 15-16] (up-left)
3. Clicked [36, 40] on id=10 (boundary [36-37, 40-41]): moved to [35-36, 39-40] (up-left)
4. Clicked [54, 49] on id=11 (boundary [53-54, 48-49]): NO MOVE
In cases 1-3, I clicked on the top-left corner of the square. In case 4, I clicked on the bottom-right corner.
So the rule seems to be: clicking on the top-left corner of a magenta square moves it up-left.
Let me test this by clicking on the top-left corner of id=11, which is [53, 48].

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (revised):
- Clicking top-left corner of magenta square moves it up-left.
- Clicking bottom-right corner [54, 49] had NO effect.
- Need to test other corners to understand full movement mechanics.
Plan: Click top-left corner [53, 48] of id=11 to confirm it moves up-left.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click top-left corner of id=11 at [53, 48]
predict(expect_change=True, note='Clicking top-left [53,48] of magenta square should move it up-left')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 53, 'col': 48}])
print("Result:", last_action_result)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Magenta nodes after click:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
